text: In the Hebrew text, the verse beginning ‘Train a boy according to the measure of his way’; but, even when this correction is made, the sense of the maxim remains uncertain.
In the second half of this verse, the Latin (not the Hebrew) would allow us to translate, ‘he will be brought to an end by the flail of his own malice’.
The sentence in brackets is found in the Septuagint Greek, but not in the Hebrew text. The sense of its second half is uncertain, but cf. Gen. 14.21.
The Latin version here gives the most intelligible account of a maxim whose meaning has been much disputed.
